book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
On the words Incarnate, and Made Man.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: On the words Incarnate, and Made Man.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: “And the Lord spoke again unto Ahaz, saying, Ask thee a sign, &c.:” and “Behold! a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call His name Emmanuel, &c.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nurslings of purity and disciples of chastity, raise we our hymn to the Virgin-born God with lips full of purity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Deemed worthy to partake of the flesh of the Spiritual Lamb, let us take the head together with the feet, the Deity being understood as the head, and the Manhood taken as the feet.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hearers of the Holy Gospels, let us listen to John the Divine.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he who said, In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God, went on to say, and the Word was made flesh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For neither is it holy to worship the mere man, nor religious to say that He is God only without the Manhood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if Christ is God, as indeed He is, but took not human nature upon Him, we are strangers to salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us then worship Him as God, but believe that He also was made Man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For neither is there any profit in calling Him man without Godhead nor any salvation in refusing to confess the Manhood together with the Godhead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us confess the presence of Him who is both King and Physician.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Jesus the King when about to become our Physician, girded Himself with the linen of humanity, and healed that which was sick.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The perfect Teacher of babes became a babe among babes, that He might give wisdom to the foolish.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Bread of heaven came down on earth that He might feed the hungry.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the sons of the Jews by setting at nought Him that came, and looking for him who cometh in wickedness, rejected the true Messiah, and wait for the deceiver, themselves deceived; herein also the Saviour being found true, who said, I am come in My Father’s name, and ye receive Me not: but if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is well also to put a question to the Jews.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is the Prophet Esaias, who saith that Emmanuel shall be born of a virgin, true or false?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if they charge him with falsehood, no wonder: for their custom is not only to charge with falsehood, but also to stone the Prophets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if the Prophet is true, point to the Emmanuel, and say, Whether is He who is to come, for whom ye are looking, to be born of a virgin or not?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if He is not to be born of a virgin, ye accuse the Prophet of falsehood: but if in Him that is to come ye expect this, why do ye reject that which has come to pass already?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let the Jews, then, be led astray, since they so will: but let the Church of God be glorified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we receive God the Word made Man in truth, not, as heretics say, of the will of man and woman, but of The Virgin and the Holy Ghost according to the Gospel, Made Man, not in seeming but in truth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that He was truly Man made of the Virgin, wait for the proper time of instruction in this Lecture, and thou shalt receive the proofs: for the error of the heretics is manifold.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And some have said that He has not been born at all of a virgin: others that He has been born, not of a virgin, but of a wife dwelling with a husband.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Others say that the Christ is not God made Man, but a man made God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For they dared to say that not He—the pre-existent Word—was made Man; but a certain man was by advancement crowned.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But remember thou what was said yesterday concerning His Godhead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Believe that He the Only-begotten Son of God—He Himself was again begotten of a Virgin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Believe the Evangelist John when he says, And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Word is eternal, Begotten of the Father Before All Worlds: but the flesh He took on Him recently for our sake.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Many contradict this, and say: “What cause was there so great, for God to come down into humanity?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And, is it at all God’s nature to hold intercourse with men?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And, is it possible for a virgin to bear, without man?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since then there is much controversy, and the battle has many forms, come, let us by the grace of Christ, and the prayers of those who are present, resolve each question.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And first let us inquire for what cause Jesus came down.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now mind not my argumentations, for perhaps thou mayest be misled but unless thou receive testimony of the Prophets on each matter, believe not what I say: unless thou learn from the Holy Scriptures concerning the Virgin, and the place, the time, and the manner, receive not testimony from man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For one who at present thus teaches may possibly be suspected: but what man of sense will suspect one that prophesied a thousand and more years beforehand?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If then thou seekest the cause of Christ’s coming, go back to the first book of the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In six days God made the world: but the world was for man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The sun however resplendent with bright beams, yet was made to give light to man, yea, and all living creatures were formed to serve us: herbs and trees were created for our enjoyment.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: All the works of creation were good, but none of these was an image of God, save man only.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The sun was formed by a mere command, but man by God’s hands: Let us make man after our image, and after our likeness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A wooden image of an earthly king is held in honour; how much more a rational image of God?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But when this the greatest of the works of creation was disporting himself in Paradise, the envy of the Devil cast him out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The enemy was rejoicing over the fall of him whom he had envied: wouldest thou have had the enemy continue to rejoice?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not daring to accost the man because of his strength, he accosted as being weaker the woman, still a virgin: for it was after the expulsion from Paradise that Adam knew his wife.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cain and Abel succeeded in the second generation of mankind: and Cain was the first murderer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards a deluge was poured abroad because of the great wickedness of men: fire came down from heaven upon the people of Sodom because of their transgression.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: After a time God chose out Israel: but Israel also turned aside, and the chosen race was wounded.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For while Moses stood before God in the mount, the people were worshipping a calf instead of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In the lifetime of Moses, the law-giver who had said, Thou shalt not commit adultery, a man dared to enter a place of harlotry and transgress.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: After Moses, Prophets were sent to cure Israel: but in their healing office they lamented that they were not able to overcome the disease, so that one of them says, Woe is me! for the godly man is perished out of the earth, and there is none that doeth right among men: and again, They are all gone out of the way, they are together became unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one: and again, Cursing and stealing, and adultery, and murder are poured out upon the land.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Their sons and their daughters they sacrificed unto devils.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They used auguries, and enchantments, and divinations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, they fastened their garments with cords, and made hangings attached to the altar.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Very great was the wound of man’s nature; from the feet to the head there was no soundness in it; none could apply mollifying ointment, neither oil, nor bandages.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then bewailing and wearying themselves, the Prophets said, Who shall give salvation out of Sion?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, Let Thy hand be upon the man of Thy right hand, and upon the son of man whom Thou madest strong for Thyself: so will not we go back from Thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And another of the Prophets entreated, saying, Bow the heavens, O Lord and come down.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The wounds of man’s nature pass our healing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They slew Thy Prophets, and cast down Thine altars.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The evil is irretrievable by us, and needs thee to retrieve it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord heard the prayer of the Prophets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Father disregarded not the perishing of our race; He sent forth His Son, the Lord from heaven, as healer: and one of the Prophets saith, The Lord whom ye seek, cometh, and shall suddenly come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord shall come to His own temple, where ye stoned Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then another of the Prophets, on hearing this, saith to him: In speaking of the salvation of God, speakest thou quietly?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In preaching the good tidings of God’s coming for salvation, speakest thou in secret?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O thou that bringest good tidings to Zion, get thee up into the high mountain.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Speak to the cities of Judah.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What am I to speak?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold our God!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold! the Lord cometh with strength!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again the Lord Himself saith, Behold!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I come, and I will dwell in the midst of thee, saith the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And many nations shall flee unto the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Israelites rejected salvation through Me: I come to gather all nations and tongues.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He came to His own and His own received Him not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou comest and what dost Thou bestow on the nations?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I come to gather all nations, and I will leave on them a sign.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For from My conflict upon the Cross I give to each of My soldiers a royal seal to bear upon his forehead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Another also of the Prophets said, He bowed the heavens also, and came down; and darkness was under His feet.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For His coming down from heaven was not known by men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards Solomon hearing his father David speak these things, built a wondrous house, and foreseeing Him who was to come into it, said in astonishment, Will God in very deed dwell with men on the earth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yea, saith David by anticipation in the Psalm inscribed For Solomon, wherein is this, He shall come down like rain into a fleece: rain, because of His heavenly nature, and into a fleece, because of His humanity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For rain, coming down into a fleece, comes down noiselessly: so that the Magi, not knowing the mystery of the Nativity, say, Where is He that is born King of the Jews? and Herod being troubled inquired concerning Him who was born, and said, Where is the Christ to be born?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But who is this that cometh down?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says in what follows, And with the sun He endureth, and before the moon generations of generations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again another of the Prophets saith, Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Sion, shout, O daughter of Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold! thy King cometh unto thee, just and having salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Kings are many; of which speakest thou, O Prophet?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Give us a sign which other Kings have not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou say, A king clad in purple, the dignity of the apparel has been anticipated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou say, Guarded by spear-men, and sitting in a golden chariot, this also has been anticipated by others.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Give us a sign peculiar to the King whose coming thou announcest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Prophet maketh answer and saith, Behold! thy King cometh unto thee, just, and having salvation: He is meek, and riding upon an ass and a young foal, not on a chariot.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast a unique sign of the King who came.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jesus alone of kings sat upon an unyoked foal, entering into Jerusalem with acclamations as a king.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when this King is come, what doth He?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou also by the blood of the covenant hast sent forth thy prisoners out of the pit wherein is no water.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But He might perchance even sit upon a foal: give us rather a sign, where the King that entereth shall stand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And give the sign not far from the city, that it may not be unknown to us: and give us the sign plain before our eyes, that even when in the city we may behold the place.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Prophet again makes answer, saying: And His feet shall stand in that day upon the Mount of Olives which is before Jerusalem on the east.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does any one standing within the city fail to behold the place?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We have two signs, and we desire to learn a third.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell us what the Lord doth when He is come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Another Prophet saith, Behold! our God, and afterwards, He will come and save us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall hear: then shall the lame man leap as an hart, and the tongue of the stammerers shall be distinct.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let yet another testimony be told us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou sayest, O Prophet, that the Lord cometh, and doeth signs such as never were: what other clear sign tellest thou?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord Himself entereth into judgment with the elders of His people, and with the princes thereof.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A notable sign!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Master judged by His servants, the elders, and submitting to it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These things the Jews read, but hear not: for they have stopped the ears of their heart, that they may not hear.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us believe in Jesus Christ, as having come in the flesh and been made Man, because we could not receive Him otherwise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For since we could not look upon or enjoy Him as He was, He became what we are, that so we might be permitted to enjoy Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if we cannot look full on the sun, which was made on the fourth day, could we behold God its Creator?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord came down in fire on Mount Sinai, and the people could not bear it, but said to Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear; and let not God speak to us, lest we die: and again, For who is there of all flesh that hath heard the voice of the living God speaking out of the midst of the fire, and shall live?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If to hear the voice of God speaking is a cause of death, how shall not the sight of God Himself bring death?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what wonder?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even Moses himself saith, I exceedingly fear and quake.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What wouldest thou then?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That He who came for our salvation should become a minister of destruction because men could not bear Him? or that He should suit His grace to our measure?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Daniel could not bear the vision of an Angel, and wert thou capable of the sight of the Lord of Angels?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Gabriel appeared, and Daniel fell down: and of what nature or in what guise was he that appeared?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His countenance was like lightning; not like the sun: and his eyes as lamps of fire, not as a furnace of fire: and the voice of his words as the voice of a multitude, not as the voice of twelve legions of angels; nevertheless the Prophet fell down.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Angel cometh unto him, saying, Fear not, Daniel, stand upright: be of good courage, thy words are heard.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Daniel says, I stood up trembling: and not even so did he make answer, until the likeness of a man’s hand touched him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when he that appeared was changed into the appearance of a man, then Daniel spoke: and what saith he?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O my Lord, at the vision of Thee my inward parts were turned within me, and no strength remaineth in me, neither is there breath left in me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If an Angel appearing took away the Prophet’s voice and strength, would the appearance of God have allowed him to breathe?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And until there touched me as it were a vision of a man, saith the Scripture, Daniel took not courage.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So then after trial shewn of our weakness, the Lord assumed that which man required: for since man required to hear from one of like countenance, the Saviour took on Him the nature of like affections, that men might be the more easily instructed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Learn also another cause.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Christ came that He might be baptized, and might sanctify Baptism: He came that He might work wonders, walking upon the waters of the sea.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since then before His appearance in flesh, the sea saw Him and fled, and Jordan was turned back, the Lord took to Himself His body, that the sea might endure the sight, and Jordan receive Him without fear.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This then is one cause; but there is also a second.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Through Eve yet virgin came death; through a virgin, or rather from a virgin, must the Life appear: that as the serpent beguiled the one, so to the other Gabriel might bring good tidings.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Men forsook God, and made carved images of men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since therefore an image of man was falsely worshipped as God, God became truly Man, that the falsehood might be done away.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Devil had used the flesh as an instrument against us; and Paul knowing this, saith, But I see another law in my members warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: By the very same weapons, therefore, wherewith the Devil used to vanquish us, have we been saved.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord took on Him from us our likeness, that He might save man’s nature: He took our likeness, that He might give greater grace to that which lacked; that sinful humanity might become partaker of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For where sin abounded, grace did much more abound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It behoved the Lord to suffer for us; but if the Devil had known Him, he would not have dared to approach Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For had they known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of Glory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His body therefore was made a bait to death that the dragon, hoping to devour it, might disgorge those also who had been already devoured.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Death prevailed and devoured; and again, God wiped away every tear from off every face.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Was it without reason that Christ was made Man?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are our teachings ingenious phrases and human subtleties?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are not the Holy Scriptures our salvation?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are not the predictions of the Prophets?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Keep then, I pray thee, this deposit undisturbed, and let none remove thee: believe that God became Man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But though it has been proved possible for Him to be made Man, yet if the Jews still disbelieve, let us hold this forth to them: What strange thing do we announce in saying that God was made Man, when yourselves say that Abraham received the Lord as a guest?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What strange thing do we announce, when Jacob says, For I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord, who ate with Abraham, ate also with us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What strange thing then do we announce?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay more, we produce two witnesses, those who stood before Lord on Mount Sinai: Moses was in a clift of the rock, and Elias was once in a clift of the rock: they being present with Him at His Transfiguration on Mount Tabor, spoke to the Disciples of His decease which fire should accomplish at Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But, as I said before, it has been proved possible for Him to be made man: and the rest of the proofs may be left for the studious to collect.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: My statement, however, promised to declare also the time of the Saviour’s and the place: and I must not go away convicted of falsehood, but rather send away the Church’s novices well assured.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us therefore inquire the time when our Lord came: because His coming is recent, and is disputed: and because Christ Jesus is the same yesterday, and to-day, and for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Moses then, the prophet, saith, A Prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me: but let that “like unto me” be reserved awhile to be examined in its proper place.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But when cometh this Prophet that is expected?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Recur, he says, to what has been written by me: examine carefully Jacob’s prophecy addressed to Judah: Judah, thee may thy brethren praise, and afterwards, not to quote the whole, A prince shall not fail out of Judah, nor a ruler from his loins, until He come, for whom it is reserved; and He is the expectation, not of the Jews but of the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He gave, therefore, as a sign of Christ’s advent the cessation of the Jewish rule.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If they are not now under the Romans, the Christ is not yet come: if they still have a prince of the race of Judah and of David, he is not yet come that was expected.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For I am ashamed to tell of their recent doings concerning those who are now called Patriarchs among them, and what their descent is, and who their mother: but I leave it to those who know.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But He that cometh as the expectation of the Gentiles, what further sign then hath He?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says next, Binding his foal unto the vine.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that foal which was clearly announced by Zachariah.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But again thou askest yet another testimony of the time.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord said unto Me, Thou art My Son; this day have I begotten Thee: and a few words further on, Thou shalt rule them with a rod of iron.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I have said before that the kingdom of the Romans is clearly called a rod of iron; but what is wanting concerning this let us further call to mind out of Daniel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in relating and interpreting to Nebuchadnezzar the image of the statue, he tells also his whole vision concerning it: and that a stone cut out of a mountain without hands, that is, not set up by human contrivance, should overpower the whole world: and he speaks most clearly thus; And in the days of those kingdoms the God of heaven shall set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed, and His kingdom shall not be left to another people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we seek still more clearly the proof of the time of His coming.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For man being hard to persuade, unless he gets the very years for a clear calculation, does not believe what is stated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then is the season, and what the manner of the time?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is when, on the failure of the kings descended from Judah, Herod a foreigner succeeds to the kingdom?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Angel, therefore, who converses with Daniel says, and do thou now mark the words, And thou shalt know and understand: From the going forth of the word for making answer, and for the building of Jerusalem, until Messiah the Prince are seven weeks and three score and two weeks.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now three score and nine weeks of years contain four hundred and eighty-three years.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He said, therefore, that after the building of Jerusalem, four hundred and eighty-three years having passed, and the rulers having failed, then cometh a certain king of another race, in whose time the Christ is to be born.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now Darius the Mede built the city in the sixth year of his own reign, and first year of the 66th Olympiad according to the Greeks.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Olympiad is the name among the Greeks of the games celebrated after four years, because of the day which in every four years of the sun’s courses is made up of the three (supernumerary) hours in each year.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Herod is king in the 186th Olympiad, in the 4th year thereof.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now from the 66th to the 186th Olympiad there are 120 Olympiads intervening, and a little over.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So then the 120 Olympiads make up 480 years: for the other three years remaining are perhaps taken up in the interval between the first and fourth years.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And there thou hast the proof according to the Scripture which saith, From the going forth of the word that Jerusalem be restored and built until Messiah the Prince are seven weeks and sixty-two weeks.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of the times, therefore, thou hast for the present this proof, although there are also other different interpretations concerning the aforesaid weeks of years in Daniel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But now hear the place of the promise, as Micah says, And thou, Bethlehem, house of Ephrathah, art thou little to be among the thousands of Judah?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For out of thee shall come forth unto Me a ruler, to be governor in Israel: and His goings forth are front the beginning, from the days of eternity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But assuredly as to the places, thou being an inhabitant of Jerusalem, knowest also beforehand what is written in the hundred and thirty-first psalm.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Lo! we heard of it at Ephrathah, we found it in the plains of the wood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For a few years ago the place was woody.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again thou hast heard Habakkuk say to the Lord, When the years draw nigh, thou shalt be made known, when the time is come, thou shalt be shewn.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what is the sign, O Prophet, of the Lord’s coming?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And presently he saith, In the midst of two lives shalt thou be known, plainly saying this to the Lord, “Having come in the flesh thou livest and diest, and after rising from the dead thou livest again.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further, from what part of the region round Jerusalem cometh He?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: From east, or west, or north, or south?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell us exactly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And he makes answer most plainly and says, God shall come from Teman (now Teman is by interpretation ‘south’) and the Holy One from Mount Paran, shady, woody: what the Psalmist spake in like words, We found it in the plains of the wood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We ask further, of whom cometh He and how?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this Esaias tells us: Behold! the virgin shall conceive in her womb, and shall bring forth a Son, and they shall call His name Emmanuel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This the Jews contradict, for of old it is their wont wickedly to oppose the truth: and they say that it is not written “the virgin,” but “the damsel.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But though I assent to what they say, even so I find the truth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we must ask them, If a virgin be forced, when does she cry out and call for helpers, after or before the outrage?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If, therefore, the Scripture elsewhere says, The betrothed damsel cried, and there was none to save her, doth it not speak of a virgin?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But that you may learn more plainly that even a virgin is called in Holy Scripture a “damsel,” hear the Book of the Kings, speaking of Abishag the Shunamite, And the damsel was very fair: for that as a virgin she was chosen and brought to David is admitted.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Jews say again, This was said to Ahaz in reference to Hezekiah.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Well, then, let us read the Scripture: Ask thee a sign of the Lord thy God, in the depth or in the height.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the sign certainly must be something astonishing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the water from the rock was a sign, the sea divided, the sun turning back, and the like.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But in what I am going to mention there is still more manifest refutation of the Jews.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: (I know that I am speaking at much length, and that my hearers are wearied: but bear with the fulness of my statements, because it is for Christ’s sake these questions are moved, and they concern no ordinary matters.)

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now as Isaiah spoke this in the reign of Ahaz, and Ahaz reigned only sixteen years, and the prophecy was spoken to him within these years, the objection of the Jews is refuted by the fact that the succeeding king, Hezekiah, son of Ahaz, was twenty-five years old when he began to reign: for as the prophecy is confined within sixteen years, he must have been begotten of Ahaz full nine years before the prophecy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What need then was there to utter the prophecy concerning one who had been already begotten even before the reign of father Ahaz?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he said not, hath conceived, but “the virgin shall conceive,” speaking as with foreknowledge.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We know then for certain that the Lord was to be born of a Virgin, but we have to shew of what family the Virgin was.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord sware in truth unto David, and will not set it aside.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of the fruit of body will I set upon thy throne: and again, seed will I establish for ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And afterwards, Once have I sworn by My holiness that I will not lie unto David.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His seed shall endure for ever, and his throne as the sun before Me, and as the moon established for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that the discourse is of Christ, not of Solomon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Solomon’s throne endured not as the sun.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if any deny this, because Christ sat not on David’s throne of wood, we will bring forward that saying, The Scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat: for it signifies not his wooden seat, but the authority of his teaching.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In like manner then I would have you seek for David’s throne not the throne of wood, but the kingdom itself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take, too, as my witnesses the children who cried aloud,Hosanna to the Son of David, blessed is the King of Israel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the blind men also say, Son of David, have mercy on us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Gabriel too testifies plainly to Mary, saying, And the Lord God shall give unto Him the throne of His father David.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Paul also saith, Remember Jesus Christ raised from the dead, of the seed of David, according to my Gospel: and in the beginning of the Epistle to the Romans he saith, Which was made of the seed of David according to the flesh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Receive thou therefore Him that was born of David, believing the prophecy which saith, And in that day there shall be a root of Jesse, and He that shall rise to rule over the Gentiles: in Him shall the Gentiles trust.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Jews are much troubled at these things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This also Isaiah foreknew, saying, And they shall wish that they had been burnt with fire: for unto us a child is born (not unto them), unto us a Son is given.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mark thou that at first He was the Son of God, then was given to us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And a little after he says, And of His peace there is no bound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Romans have bounds: of the kingdom of the Son of God there is no bound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Persians and the Medes have bounds, but the Son has no bound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then next, upon the throne of David, and upon his kingdom to order it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Holy Virgin, therefore, is from David.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it became Him who is most pure, and a teacher of purity, to have come forth from a pure bride-chamber.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if he who well fulfils the office of a priest of Jesus abstains from a wife, how should Jesus Himself be born of man and woman?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For thou, saith He in the Psalms, art He that took Me out of the womb.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mark that carefully, He that took Me out of the womb, signifying that He was begotten without man, being taken from a virgin’s womb and flesh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the manner is different with those who are begotten according to the course of marriage.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And from such members He is not ashamed to assume flesh, who is the framer of those very members.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But then who telleth us this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord saith unto Jeremiah: Before I formed thee in the belly, I knew thee: and before thou camest forth out of the womb, I sanctified thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If, then, in fashioning man He was not ashamed of the contact, was He ashamed in fashioning for His own sake the holy Flesh, the veil of His Godhead?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is God who even now creates the children in the womb, as it is written in Job, Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast knit me together with bones and sinews.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There is nothing polluted in the human frame except a man defile this with fornication and adultery.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who formed Adam formed Eve also, and male and female were formed by God’s hands.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: None of the members of the body as formed from the beginning is polluted.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let the mouths of all heretics be stopped who slander their bodies, or rather Him who formed them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us remember Paul’s saying, Know ye not that your bodies are the temples of the Holy Ghost which is in you?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again the Prophet hath spoken before in the person of Jesus, My flesh is from them: and in another place it is written, Therefore will He give them up, until the time that she bringeth forth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what is the sign?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He tells us in what follows, She shall bring forth, and the remnant of their brethren shall return.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what are the nuptial pledges of the Virgin, the holy bride?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And I will betroth thee unto Me in faithfulness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Elizabeth, talking with Mary, speaks in like manner: And blessed is she that believed; for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But both Greeks and Jews harass us and say that it was impossible for the Christ to be born of a virgin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As for the Greeks we will stop their mouths from their own fables.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For ye who say that stones being thrown were changed into men, how say ye that it is impossible for a virgin to bring forth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye who fable that a daughter was born from the brain, how say ye that it is impossible for a son to have been born from a virgin’s womb?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye who falsely say that Dionysus was born from the thigh of your Zeus, how set ye at nought our truth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I know that I am speaking of things unworthy of the present audience: but in order that thou in due season mayest rebuke the Greeks, we have brought these things forward answering them from their own fables.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But those of the circumcision meet thou with this question: Whether is harder, for an aged woman, barren and past age, to bear, or for a virgin in the prime of youth to conceive?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Sarah was barren, and though it had ceased to be with her after the manner of women, yet, contrary to nature, she bore a child.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If, then, it is against nature for a barren woman to conceive, and also for a virgin, either, therefore, reject both, or accept both.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is the same God who both wrought the one and appointed the other.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For thou wilt not dare to say that it was possible for God in that former case, and impossible in this latter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again: how is it natural for a man’s hand to be changed in a single hour into a different appearance and restored again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How then was the hand of Moses made white as snow, and at once restored again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou sayest that God’s will made the change.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In that case God’s will has the power, and has it then no power in this case?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That moreover was a sign concerning the Egyptians only, but this was a sign given to the whole world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But whether is the more difficult, O ye Jews?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For a virgin to bear, or for a rod to be quickened into a living creature?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye confess that in the case of Moses a perfectly straight rod became like a serpent, and was terrible to him who cast it down, and he who before held the rod fast, fled from it as from a serpent; for a serpent in truth it was: but he fled not because he feared that which he held, but because he dreaded Him that had changed it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A rod had teeth and eyes like a serpent: do then seeing eyes grow out of a rod, and cannot a child be born of a virgin’s womb, if God wills?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For I say nothing of the fact that Aaron’s rod also produced in a single night what other trees produce in several years.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For who knows not that a rod, after losing its bark, will never sprout, not even if it be planted in the midst of rivers?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since God is not dependent on the nature of trees, but is the Creator of their natures, the unfruitful, and dry, and barkless rod budded, and blossomed, and bare almonds.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He, then, who for the sake of the typical high-priest gave fruit supernaturally to the rod, would He not for the sake of the true High-Priest grant to the Virgin to bear a child?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These are excellent suggestions of the narratives: but the Jews still contradict, and do not yield to the statements concerning the rod, unless they may be persuaded by similar strange and supernatural births.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Question them, therefore, in this way: of whom in the beginning was Eve begotten?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What mother conceived her the motherless?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Scripture saith that she was born out of Adam’s side.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is Eve then born out of a man’s side without a mother, and is a child not to be born without a father, of a virgin’s womb?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This debt of gratitude was due to men from womankind: for Eve was begotten of Adam, and not conceived of a mother, but as it were brought forth of man alone.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mary, therefore, paid the debt, of gratitude, when not by man but of herself alone in an immaculate way she conceived of the Holy Ghost by the power of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us take what is yet a greater wonder than this.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For that of bodies bodies should be conceived, even if wonderful, is nevertheless possible: but that the dust of the earth should become a man, this is more wonderful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That clay moulded together should assume the coats and splendours of the eyes, this is more wonderful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That out of dust of uniform appearance should be produced both the firmness of bones, and the softness of lungs, and other different kinds of members, this is wonderful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That clay should be animated and travel round the world self moved, and should build houses, this is wonderful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That clay should teach, and talk, and act as carpenter, and as king, this is wonderful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Whence, then, O ye most ignorant Jews, was Adam made?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Did not God take dust from the earth, and fashion this wonderful frame?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is then clay changed into an eye, and cannot a virgin bear a son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does that which for men is more impossible take place, and is that which is possible never to occur?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us remember these things, brethren: let us use these weapons in our defence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not endure those heretics who teach Christ’s coming as a phantom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us abhor those also who say that the Saviour’s birth was of husband and wife; who have dared to say that He was the child of Joseph and Mary, because it is written, And he took unto him his wife.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For let us remember Jacob who before he received Rachel, said to Laban, Give me my wife.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as she before the wedded state, merely because there was a promise, was called the wife of Jacob, so also Mary, because she had been betrothed, was called the wife of Joseph.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mark also the accuracy of the Gospel, saying, And in the sixth month the Angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth, to a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, and so forth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again when the census took place, and Joseph went up to enrol himself, what saith the Scripture?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, to enrol himself with Mary who was espoused to him, being great with child.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though she was with child, yet it said not “with his wife,” but with her who was espoused to him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God sent forth His Son, says Paul, not made of a man and a woman, but made of a woman only, that is of a virgin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For that the virgin also is called a woman, we shewed before.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He who makes souls virgin, was born of a Virgin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou wonderest at the event: even she herself who bare him wondered at this.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For she saith to Gabriel, How shall this be to me, since I know not a man?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But he says, The Holy Ghost shall came upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: wherefore also the holy thing which is to be born shall be called the Son of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Immaculate and undefiled was His generation: for where the Holy Spirit breathes, there all pollution is taken away: undefiled from the Virgin was the incarnate generation of the Only-begotten.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if the heretics gainsay the truth, the Holy Ghost shall convict them: that overshadowing power of the Highest shall wax wroth: Gabriel shall stand face to face against them in the day of judgment: the place of the manger, which received the Lord, shall put them to shame.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The shepherds, who then received the good tidings, shall bear witness; and the host of the Angels who sang praises and hymns, and said, Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among men of His good pleasure: the Temple into which He was then carried up on the fortieth day: the pairs of turtle-doves, which were offered on His behalf: and Symeon who then took Him up in his arms, and Anna the prophetess who was present.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since God then beareth witness, and the Holy Ghost joins in the witness, and Christ says, Why do ye seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth? let the heretics be silenced who speak against His humanity, for they speak against Him, who saith, Handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Adored be the Lord the Virgin-born, and let Virgins acknowledge the crown of their own state: let the order also of Solitaries acknowledge the glory of chastity for we men are not deprived of the dignity of chastity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In the Virgin’s womb the Saviour’s period of nine months was passed: but the Lord was for thirty and three years a man: so that if a virgin glories because of the nine months, much more we because of the many years.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us all by God’s grace run the race of chastity, young men and maidens, old men and children; not going after wantonness, but praising the name of Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not be ignorant of the glory of chastity: for its crown is angelic, and its excellence above man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us be chary of our bodies which are to shine as the sun: let us not for short pleasure defile so great, so noble a body: for short and momentary is the sin, but the shame for many years and for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Angels walking upon earth are they who practise chastity: the Virgins have their portion with Mary the Virgin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let all vain ornament be banished, and every hurtful glance, and all wanton gait, and every flowing robe, and perfume enticing to pleasure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But in all for perfume let there be the prayer of sweet odour, and the practice of good works, and the sanctification of our bodies: that the Virgin-born Lord may say even of us, both men who live in chastity and women who wear the crown, I will dwell in them; and walk in them, and I will be their God, and they shall be My people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To whom be the glory for ever and ever.
